title: Ryan Realty Asset Library — Asset Schema
description: Schema for entries in data/asset-library/manifest.json. Every photo, video, audio file, or render that the content engine touches gets one of these.

type.description: What kind of asset. 'render' = our produced output (mp4 from Remotion); the others = inputs we fetched or generated.

source.description: Where this asset came from. 'curated' = Matt-approved, 'render-output' = our own deliverable.

source_id.description: Source's own ID for the asset (Unsplash photo_id, Shutterstock id, Replicate prediction id, etc.)

license_metadata.description: License-specific tracking. e.g. { shutterstock_license_id: '123', license_cost_usd: 1.50, license_purchased_at: '...' }

creator.description: Photographer name, AI model name, etc.

file_path.description: Path relative to repo root, e.g. public/asset-library/photos/shutterstock/abc123.jpg

file_url.description: Public URL if stored remotely (Supabase Storage, CDN, etc.)

geo_tags.description: City / neighborhood / landmark tags. Lowercase kebab-case. e.g. ['bend', 'central-oregon', 'smith-rock', 'tumalo-falls']

search_query.description: What query was used to find/generate this asset (helps trace provenance)

used_in.description: Track every render that used this asset. Builds a usage history.

scene_id.description: e.g. 'stat-3' or 'intro' — the beat that used it

approval.description: Asset library approval state. Only 'approved' assets enter production renders. 'intake' = waiting for Matt's review.

notes.description: Free-form notes (e.g. 'best for hero, north-facing, snow-capped peak')
